card.intuition: Build an ensemble of random isolation trees: at each node, pick a random feature and a random split value between the feature's min and max. Anomalies are isolated near the root (short path length) because they occupy sparse regions; normal points buried deep in dense regions require many splits to isolate. Anomaly score = 2^(-avg_path / c(n)) where c(n) is the expected path length for n samples.
card.analogy: Finding a needle in a haystack vs finding a person: the needle is isolated from other objects so random cuts quickly separate it. A person in a crowd takes many cuts to single out. Anomalies are needles — they're isolated quickly; normal points are people in crowds.
card.time_complexity: O(n · t · log n) to build t trees of depth log n; O(t · log n) per query
when_to_use: Unsupervised anomaly detection without labelled data
High-dimensional data where density estimation is expensive
Fast inference needed — each prediction is just averaging path lengths
gotchas: Contamination parameter sets the expected fraction of anomalies — tune with domain knowledge
Masking effect: dense clusters of anomalies may appear normal — use Extended Isolation Forest for robustness
Path length is averaged over all trees — more trees (default 100) reduce variance but increase cost

exercise.prompt: Implement a minimal Isolation Tree and compute average path length for anomaly scoring.
hints: At a leaf with size s, the point couldn't be further isolated — its effective path length is depth + c(s), where c(s) adjusts for the unbuilt subtree below
exercise.test_code:
random.seed(42)
# Normal cluster around origin, one anomaly far away
X_normal = [[random.gauss(0,1), random.gauss(0,1)] for _ in range(100)]
X_anom   = [[10.0, 10.0]]
X_all    = X_normal + X_anom

scores = anomaly_score(X_all, X_all, n_trees=100, max_depth=8)

# Anomaly (last point) should have higher score than median of normals
import statistics
normal_scores = scores[:100]
med = statistics.median(normal_scores)
assert scores[100] > med, f'Anomaly score {scores[100]:.4f} should exceed median {med:.4f}'
# Most normal points should score below 0.6
assert sum(s < 0.6 for s in normal_scores) > 70, 'Most normal points should have low anomaly score'
print('PASS')
